decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). To address the violation conservatively, I will increase the heat spreader width to improve lateral heat spreading area without hitting material property limits.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width expands the cross-sectional area for lateral heat conduction, reducing thermal resistance and lowering the peak chip temperature. This is a geometric adjustment that avoids the material conductivity ceiling encountered previously.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
Improved feasibility regarding the chip_max_temperature constraint
risk_notes: Widening the spreader approaches the design domain width limit (1.2m); future width increases may be constrained.
Geometric changes may require mesh adaptation if the aspect ratio becomes extreme, though the current step is within safe bounds.
